frameworks.name: interdisciplinary cognitive science
frameworks.one_line: Cognition as a complex phenomenon requiring synthesis of diverse disciplines.

frameworks.name: complexity science
frameworks.one_line: Complex phenomena beckon convergent input across many disciplines.

frameworks.name: cybernetics (Macy Conferences legacy)
frameworks.one_line: 1940s–50s cybernetics framed as groundwork for cognitive science.

core_question: How should cognitive science navigate the enduring tension between the challenges and benefits of interdisciplinary integration?
key_assumptions: Cognition is intrinsically complex and resists single-discipline accounts.
Interdisciplinary integration is constitutive of cognitive science's identity.
interpretations: The challenge–benefit tension of interdisciplinary work traces back to the field's cybernetic roots.
out_of_scope_note: Editorial introduction to a special issue; reflective/historical commentary on cognitive science as an interdisciplinary field, not an empirical research paper.
